scene.description: A muddy trailhead at the edge of town, rain still soft in the air.

stages.situation: The road out of town is thin and wet. Somewhere past the treeline the rain is heavier. You could go now, or wait it out.

choices.text: Go now, while the rain is only a whisper.
choices.outcome: You walk into it. The rain finds the back of your collar within the hour, but the road is already behind you.

choices.text: Wait under the eaves for it to ease.
choices.outcome: You wait longer than you meant to. By the time you leave, the sky has thinned to a pale grey and the road is quieter for it.

choices.text: Cut through the wet field to save the hour.
choices.outcome: The field is slower than it looks, and colder. You come out the other side with mud to the knee and a chill you can't quite shake.

scene.description: A fork in the road beside a stone well, blossom just starting on the trees.

stages.situation: A woman is resting against the well, pack half-open beside her. She looks up as you near and says nothing, waiting to see if you'll stop.

choices.text: Ask if she wants company on the road.
choices.outcome: She looks at you for a long moment, then nods once and shoulders her pack. "Mira," she says, like that settles it.

choices.text: Share what food you can spare, then walk on alone.
choices.memory: A stranger's quiet thanks at a well you never learned the name of.
choices.outcome: She eats slowly, watching the road rather than you. When you leave she raises a hand, and you carry that small weight with you.

choices.text: Nod and keep walking.
choices.outcome: She doesn't call after you. The well disappears behind a bend, and the road narrows again.

scene.description: A shallow stream crossing under new leaves, sunlight breaking through in patches.

stages.situation: The stream is cold and only knee-deep. On the far bank, something pale catches the light among the stones.

choices.text: Sit on the bank a while before crossing.
choices.outcome: The water talks to itself over the stones. You feel some of the last few days loosen in your shoulders.

choices.text: Wade over and see what's catching the light.
choices.memory: A smooth river stone, pale as bone, pocketed without knowing why.
choices.outcome: It's only a stone, smoothed pale by years of current. You take it anyway.

choices.text: Cross quickly and keep the pace.
choices.outcome: You're across before the cold really reaches you, and the road climbs away from the water on the other side.

scene.description: An old footbridge over a gorge, planks silvered and loose in places.

stages.situation: The bridge groans under its own weight before you've even set foot on it. A longer path winds down and around the gorge instead.

choices.text: Cross quickly, before you can think better of it.
choices.unavailable_reason: your legs won't hold a run like that right now
choices.outcome: The planks complain the whole way, but they hold. You don't look down until you're across.

choices.text: Cross slowly, testing each board.
choices.outcome: It takes twice as long as it should. Somewhere in the middle you stop trusting the bridge and start trusting your own feet instead.

choices.text: Take the longer path around the gorge.
choices.outcome: The detour costs you the afternoon, but the ground under you never once feels uncertain.

scene.description: The last blossoms of the season scattering across a hillside track.

stages.situation: A young herbalist is crouched in the grass, gathering something into a cloth pouch. She glances up without much surprise, as if she'd heard you coming a while ago.

choices.text: Offer to travel together a while.
choices.outcome: "Only if you don't mind stopping for weeds," she says, already tying the pouch to her belt.

choices.text: Ask what she's gathering.
choices.memory: The name of a small white flower, already half-forgotten.
choices.outcome: She tells you, and you forget the name within the hour, but not the way she said it — like it mattered.

choices.text: Leave her to her work and keep walking.
choices.outcome: The hillside evens out ahead of you, and somewhere behind, summer is already arriving without you.

scene.description: A dry, sunbaked plain shimmering at the edges, a lone tree offering the only shade for miles.

stages.situation: The heat presses down harder than you expected. The tree ahead throws a thin patch of shade, and beyond it the road just keeps going, pale and shimmering.

choices.text: Rest in the shade until the worst of the heat passes.
choices.outcome: You wait it out, watching the heat ripple over the plain. It's slow, but you leave steadier than you arrived.

choices.text: Push through while there's still light left.
choices.outcome: The sun doesn't let up once. By the time the plain ends, your shirt is stiff with dried salt.

choices.text: Share water with a traveler slumped by the roadside.
choices.memory: A stranger's hand, shaking, closing around a water skin.
choices.outcome: He doesn't say much, just drinks like it's the first water he's seen in a day. Maybe it is.

scene.description: A dusty crossroads market, awnings flapping in the heat, voices calling out prices.

stages.situation: A trader with a limp and a quick laugh falls into step beside you at the edge of the market, in no apparent hurry to sell you anything.

choices.text: Ask if she'd rather walk than sell.

companion.name: Talia
companion.one_line_trait: talks to fill silences, means well by it

choices.outcome: "Finally," she says, like she'd been waiting for the offer the whole conversation. "Talia. You'll get used to me."

choices.text: Trade what you can spare for dried supplies.
choices.outcome: The trader haggles out of habit more than need. You leave with your pack heavier and your pockets lighter.

choices.text: Keep to the edge of the market and move on.
choices.outcome: The noise of the market fades behind you, and the road opens back out into quiet, dry fields.

scene.description: A cracked riverbed, the water reduced to a thin, muddy trickle.

stages.situation: The heat hasn't broken in days. Your legs feel heavier than the distance should account for.

choices.text: Move fast to clear the riverbed before dark.
choices.unavailable_reason: you don't have that kind of pace left in you
choices.outcome: You make it, but only just. Your own heartbeat is the loudest thing on the whole plain.

choices.text: Keep a slow, steady pace and drink sparingly.
choices.outcome: It's not fast, but it's honest walking. The far bank arrives right about when you expect it to.

choices.text: Make camp early and let the heat break overnight.
choices.outcome: The night cools faster than the day promised. You sleep better than you have in a while.

scene.description: A shaded orchard at the edge of a quiet village, fruit heavy on low branches.

stages.situation: The orchard isn't fenced, and nobody's in sight. The village beyond looks like it could spare a proper meal, for the right price.

choices.text: Take only what the orchard won't miss.
choices.outcome: You eat well and leave the rest. Whoever tends this place will never know you passed through.

choices.text: Pay for a proper meal in the village.
choices.outcome: It's the first hot meal in days, and it does more for you than the food alone should.

choices.text: Buy something small and useless from a stall, just to remember this place.
choices.memory: A carved wooden bird, bought for no reason but that it caught the light.
choices.outcome: It's not worth what you paid for it. You keep it anyway.

scene.description: A ridge overlooking the valley, the first hint of amber creeping into the green below.

stages.situation: From up here you can see how far you've come, and just how far is left. Someone in your company points it out before you can.

choices.text: Sit and talk a while before the descent.
choices.outcome: Nobody says anything important, and somehow that's exactly what the moment needed.

choices.text: Start down into the valley without lingering.
choices.outcome: The path down is steep and fast, and summer falls away behind you almost too easily.

choices.text: Take a moment alone at the ridge's edge.
choices.memory: The exact color of the valley the day summer started to end.
choices.outcome: You don't tell anyone why you stopped. Some things are worth keeping just for yourself.

scene.description: A forest track thick with fallen leaves, the canopy overhead gone amber and gold.

stages.situation: The valley floor is quieter than summer let it be. Somewhere under the leaves, the path has gone soft and uncertain.

choices.text: Pick your way through slowly, watching your footing.
choices.outcome: It's slow going, but you never once lose the path beneath the drift of leaves.

choices.text: Hurry through before the light goes.
choices.outcome: You misjudge a root twice and catch yourself both times. The light holds, barely.

choices.text: Stop to gather a handful of fallen leaves.
choices.memory: A single amber leaf, pressed flat between two pages.
choices.outcome: It's a small, foolish thing to carry. You carry it anyway.

scene.description: A woodcutter's clearing, smoke rising thin from a low chimney.

stages.situation: An old man is stacking wood with the unhurried care of someone who has nowhere left to be. He nods at you like he already knows why you've stopped.

choices.text: Ask if he'd walk with you a while.
choices.outcome: He looks at the woodpile, then at the road, and sets down the axe. "About time somebody asked," he says.

choices.text: Help him finish stacking the wood before moving on.
choices.outcome: He thanks you with a sack of provisions rather than words, which suits you both fine.

choices.text: Nod back and keep to the road.
choices.outcome: The smoke from his chimney is the last thing you see before the trees close back in.

scene.description: A harvested field, stubble gold under a low, cooling sun.

stages.situation: The field has already given up its harvest. What's left is quiet and spent, and the road cuts straight across it toward a treeline turning bare.

choices.text: Make an early camp at the field's edge.
choices.outcome: The stubble is dry and the ground is flat, and you sleep better than the setting deserves.

choices.text: Glean what the harvest left behind.
choices.outcome: It's not much, but it's honest food, and nobody begrudges you for taking it.

choices.text: Cross the field quickly and keep moving.
choices.outcome: The treeline swallows the last of the daylight not long after you reach it.

scene.description: A narrow valley road, cold mist pooling low between the hills.

stages.situation: The mist hasn't lifted all morning, and it's harder to shake the tiredness than it should be. Somewhere ahead, a healer's lantern is lit outside a small stone house.

choices.text: Stop at the healer's house.
choices.outcome: She doesn't ask many questions, just puts a cup of something bitter in your hands and waits until it's gone.

choices.text: Rest by the roadside instead.
choices.outcome: It's not the same as a proper bed, but the mist thins a little while you sit, and that helps more than expected.

choices.text: Push through the mist without stopping.
choices.unavailable_reason: you can barely keep pace as it is
choices.outcome: The mist never really breaks. You come out the other side more tired than the distance explains.

scene.description: The last bare trees before the road climbs toward higher, colder ground.

stages.situation: The wind carries the first real edge of winter in it. Whatever's left in your pack will need to last a while yet.

choices.text: Spend the day preparing gear for the cold ahead.
choices.outcome: It costs you a day you'd rather not lose, but your pack is heavier with things that will matter soon.

choices.text: Press on toward the high ground before the weather turns.
choices.outcome: You beat the worst of it, but only just. The first flurries catch you within sight of the ridge.

choices.text: Stop and look back at the road already walked.
choices.memory: The whole autumn valley, seen once more before the climb.
choices.outcome: From here the whole valley looks smaller than it felt at the time. You turn back to the climb without regret.

scene.description: The first real snowfall, settling quiet and white over a narrow mountain pass.

stages.situation: The pass is exposed to the wind, and the snow is already deep enough to slow you down. A half-collapsed shelter offers some cover, if you're willing to lose the time.

choices.text: Take the time to shelter properly from the wind.
choices.outcome: It's cramped and cold, but the walls hold the worst of the wind out, and that's enough.

choices.text: Keep moving without stopping for shelter.
choices.outcome: The wind finds every gap in your coat. By the far side of the pass, your fingers have stopped answering you properly.

choices.text: Duck behind a rock ledge just long enough to catch your breath.
choices.outcome: It's not real shelter, but it's something. You go back out into the snow a little steadier.

scene.description: A frozen creek bed threading between snow-heavy pines.

stages.situation: The cold has settled into your hands more than you'd like to admit. Somewhere ahead, smoke suggests a fire, if you're willing to go looking for it.

choices.text: Find the fire and warm your hands properly.
choices.outcome: Whoever built it is long gone, but the fire's still going. You sit with it longer than you mean to.

choices.text: Keep walking and ignore the cold in your hands.
choices.outcome: It's easier to ignore than it should be, which is its own kind of warning.

choices.text: Stop just long enough to wrap your hands better.
choices.outcome: It's a small fix, but it buys you the rest of the day without the cold winning outright.

scene.description: A near-empty supply depot buried in drifted snow, shelves mostly bare.

stages.situation: What's left in your pack is thin, and the depot ahead doesn't look like it has much to spare either. Whatever you decide here, it will need to carry you further than it looks capable of.

choices.text: Ration carefully and move on without stopping.
choices.outcome: You make it stretch further than seems fair. It won't last forever, but it lasts today.

choices.text: Ask the depot keeper for whatever charity allows.
choices.outcome: He gives you less than you hoped and more than he can afford, and neither of you mentions it again.

choices.text: Rest here a full day before continuing.
choices.outcome: It costs you supplies you can't really spare, but you leave standing straighter than you arrived.

scene.description: A quiet frozen lake, the ice thick enough to walk, footprints of someone else already crossing it.

stages.situation: A figure is crouched at the far edge of the ice, mending a torn pack with fingers that look nearly as cold as yours. She doesn't look up until you're close.

choices.text: Offer to travel the last stretch together.

companion.name: Wren
companion.one_line_trait: quiet, but stays close when it matters

choices.outcome: "Wren," she says, tying off the last stitch. "I wasn't planning on company, but I won't say no to it."

choices.text: Help her mend the pack before moving on.
choices.memory: A stranger's torn pack, and the cold, careful work of fixing it together.
choices.outcome: Between the two of you it doesn't take long. She thanks you plainly and turns back to her own road.

choices.text: Cross the ice and leave her to her mending.
choices.outcome: The ice holds, same as it held for her. By the far shore, she's already out of sight behind you.

scene.description: The road's end, a quiet white field opening where the path simply stops.

stages.situation: There's no gate, no marker, nothing to announce it — the road just runs out into open snow. Whatever this journey has been, it ends somewhere in this field.

choices.text: Sit down at the road's end before going any further.
choices.outcome: You let yourself stop, properly, for what feels like the first time since spring. The snow keeps falling regardless.

choices.text: Turn and look back the way you came.
choices.memory: The whole road, spring to winter, seen once more before it's only ever behind you.
choices.outcome: From here you can't see the beginning anymore. You're not sure you need to.

choices.text: Keep walking into the snow, past where the road gave out.
choices.outcome: There's nothing marking an end here, so you simply stop walking when it feels like enough. It does, eventually.
